steps.title: Recognize the contrasting structure
steps.content: The sentence uses a pivotal “but” to signal a contrast: “none had more than 2 in 1980, but ______.” The blank must show what changed by a later year — a direct reversal of the 1980 ceiling.

steps.title: Match the data to the contrast
steps.content:
Choice A (‘in 2010, Peru had 3 women on its high courts, the Philippines had 3, and Mexico had 3.’) states that in 2010, Peru had 3, the Philippines had 3, and Mexico had 3.

This directly answers the “but” by showing all three countries exceeded the 2-woman maximum from 1980, providing concrete, specific numbers from the graph that immediately support the opening claim about a trend toward more women.

The other choices either analyze patterns of change (B and C) or cover only one country, none of which complete the straightforward contrast as directly and clearly as A does.

That confirms A is correct.
